summary: Agent security, memory, and runtime infrastructure dominate today's signals. Notable: Prismor runtime security, OpenClaw SOC automation, memory projects (Tree-Ring, Mnestic, StateFuse), and MCP server security scanning.

candidates.title: Prismor: Runtime security for AI agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Blocks dangerous commands, secret leaks, prompt injection, and risky packages with policy enforcement.

candidates.title: OpenClaw Autopilot: SOC automation with MCP
candidates.why_it_matters: Automates alert triage and incident response using agents and MCP for security ops.

candidates.title: Tree-Ring Memory: Framework-agnostic memory lifecycle
candidates.why_it_matters: Local-first, SQLite-based memory with recall, forgetting, audit, and consolidation for agents.

candidates.title: Mnestic: Embedded graph+vector+FTS database for agent memory
candidates.why_it_matters: CozoDB fork tuned as agentic memory substrate with datalog support.

candidates.title: StateFuse: Deterministic conflict-preserving memory for multi-agent
candidates.why_it_matters: New arXiv paper on multi-agent memory with deterministic conflict handling.

candidates.title: Aguara: Security scanning for MCP servers and agent skills
candidates.why_it_matters: Scans AI agent skills and MCP servers for prompt injection, data leaks, and supply-chain threats.

candidates.title: AgentWeaver: Sandboxed agent with MCP server and human review gate
candidates.why_it_matters: Runs agents in git worktree sandbox, streams steps live, gates merges behind human review.

candidates.title: DeepSeek-Infra: Local-first agent infrastructure platform
candidates.why_it_matters: LLM Gateway, Agent DAG Runtime, MCP Tool Hub, A2A Agent Mesh, Tool Sandbox, Observability.

candidates.title: Desktop Touch MCP: Windows computer-use via MCP
candidates.why_it_matters: Drives desktop apps with semantic targeting and per-action perception guards.

candidates.title: QuantGist MCP server for macro event tools
candidates.why_it_matters: Exposes QuantGist macro event tools to Claude and AI agents via MCP.

candidates.title: Selfevals: Self-improving evals framework for agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Agents improve their own evaluations over time via self-supervised learning.

candidates.title: Memory Sidecar v3.5.1: Operational hardening for agent memory
candidates.why_it_matters: Agent-agnostic memory layer with production hardening, from Nous Research.

gaps: No evaluation frameworks or benchmarks captured from public sources (e.g., new agent eval libraries).
Missing signals from storage/infrastructure layers for agent data persistence.
Limited coverage of agent runtime deployment channels (e.g., new Docker images, serverless frameworks).
